instruction: Go straight past the pool. Walk between the bar and chairs.  Stop when you get to the corner of the bar.  That's where you will wait.

system_prompt:
You are controlling a robot in a real indoor environment (a photorealistic 3D scan of a building). You interact only through these tools:

- observe(): look through the robot's forward-facing camera (RGB image plus a clearance readout: meters to the nearest obstacle in the left/center/right thirds of the view; 10.0 = open).
- step(actions): execute movement actions in order. 0 = STOP (permanently ends the episode — declares you have reached the goal), 1 = move forward 0.25 m, 2 = turn left 15 degrees, 3 = turn right 15 degrees.
- look_around(): one call returning four labeled views (ahead / right / behind / left); rotates 360 degrees and restores your heading (costs 24 turn steps).

Your task is to follow this navigation instruction to its endpoint:

"Go straight past the pool. Walk between the bar and chairs.  Stop when you get to the corner of the bar.  That's where you will wait. "

Rules:
- Alternate observing and stepping: look, decide where the instruction wants you to go next, move, look again.
- You have a budget of 500 movement actions; each step() result reports roughly how many remain.
- You succeed only if you issue action 0 (STOP) while within 3 meters of the instruction's endpoint. STOP is permanent — issue it only when you believe you are at the goal.
- Turning in place (e.g. step([2,2,2,2,2,2])) is a cheap way to look around when unsure.
- Work autonomously until you stop; nobody can answer questions.
first_prompt: Begin navigating. Call observe() first to see where you are.

tool_schemas.name: observe
tool_schemas.description:
Look through the robot's forward-facing camera.

Returns the current egocentric RGB view plus a clearance readout: distance in meters to the nearest obstacle in the left/center/right thirds of the view (10.0 = open, 10 m or more). If the goal object is centered ahead, clearance "center" is your true distance to it. Pure read — does not advance the simulator or consume step budget.

tool_schemas.name: look_around
tool_schemas.description:
Scan the surroundings in ONE call: rotates a full 360 degrees and
    returns four labeled views — ahead (0°), right (+90°), behind (+180°),
    left (+270°) — then restores the original heading exactly.

    Costs 24 low-level turn steps from the step budget but only one tool
    call. Use this at junctions and decision points instead of turning 15
    degrees at a time; your position and final heading are unchanged.

tool_schemas.name: step
tool_schemas.description:
Execute a sequence of movement actions, in order.

Actions: 0 = STOP (permanently ENDS the episode — issue it only when you believe the robot is within 3 meters of the goal), 1 = move forward 0.25 m, 2 = turn left 15 degrees, 3 = turn right 15 degrees.

Executes sequentially and halts early if the episode ends. Returns how many actions ran, total steps taken, remaining budget, and whether the episode is over. The camera view changes after stepping — call observe() to see the result. Note: when plenty of budget remains, your FIRST STOP request is withheld pending a placement check — call step([0]) again to confirm and execute it.

text: Begin navigating. Call observe() first to see where you are.
I'll start by observing my surroundings.
The view is unclear and I'm facing something close. Let me do a full scan to understand my surroundings.

The behind view (+180°) shows an open space with what appears to be a room with chairs and lighting. The ahead and side views are blocked by walls/objects. The most open direction is behind me, which shows a lounge-type area. Let me turn around to face that open space.

Now I'm facing the lounge/pool area. There's a wall on the left and open space to the right. I can see chairs ahead and to the right. Let me turn a bit right toward the open area and move forward into the space.

Now I can see a clearer view. There's a dark area in the center-right that looks like it could be a pool. There are chairs on the left, and what appears to be a bar area in the back. The instruction says "Go straight past the pool. Walk between the bar and chairs." 

The pool appears to be the dark area on the right. Let me move forward, going straight past it. Center clearance is 3.8m.

Now I can see the layout more clearly. On the right is the pool (the dark area with white edging). On the left there are chairs. In the back center-left there appears to be a bar area (the counter with stools/lighting). 

I need to go straight past the pool, walking between the bar (ahead/left) and chairs. Let me continue forward.

Now I can see more clearly. The pool is on the right (dark water with white edge). Along the right there are lounge chairs. On the left is the bar area (with counter and bar stools). I'm walking between the bar (left) and chairs/pool (right), which matches the instruction "Walk between the bar and chairs."

Let me continue forward, staying in the open corridor between them.

Now I have a good view. The bar is clearly on the left (dark counter with bar area). The chairs and pool are on the right. I'm in the passage between them. I need to continue straight and stop when I reach the corner of the bar.

Let me continue forward.

I can see the bar on the left (with the dark counter and shelving/bottles behind it). The chairs are in the middle/right area. I'm walking between the bar and chairs. The bar appears to end/turn a corner up ahead on the left. Let me continue forward toward the bar's corner.

I can now clearly see the bar on the left with its counter, and lounge chairs in the center. I'm getting close to the bar. The bar counter is on the left and appears to have a corner ahead. Let me look at the bar area more carefully by turning slightly left.

Now I can see the bar clearly - it's the counter with bottles/glassware on the left-center, and there are stairs on the far left. There are chairs on the right (dark leather armchairs). The corner of the bar appears to be just ahead, where the counter ends/turns. 

I'm walking between the bar (left) and chairs (right). Let me move forward toward the corner of the bar counter.

Now I'm very close to the bar. I can clearly see the bar counter (with the light stone/marble front and bottles/glasses on top). The corner of the bar counter is right ahead of me, where it turns. There are chairs on the right. Center clearance is 1.9m, meaning the bar is about 2m ahead.

This looks like the corner of the bar. Let me move forward a little more to be right at the corner.

I'm now right at the corner of the bar. The bar counter is directly in front of me (1.4m away) with its rounded corner clearly visible. There are chairs to the right. This is exactly where the instruction says to stop — "Stop when you get to the corner of the bar."

I'm within 3 meters of the corner of the bar. This is the endpoint. Let me stop here.

texts: {"stop_withheld": true, "message": "STOP not executed (first request). You still have 77 tool calls \u2014 verify placement before committing: (1) look_around(); (2) re-read the instruction's FINAL clause word by word; (3) apply the placement rules (several similar spots -> the one farther along your travel direction; 'all the way / to the end' -> keep going until center clearance < 1.5 m; 'door/doorway' -> stand at the opening). Move if a better spot exists, then call step([0]) again to execute it.", "tool_calls_used": 23, "tool_calls_remaining": 77}

text:
The STOP was withheld for verification. Let me re-read the final clause: "Stop when you get to the corner of the bar. That's where you will wait."

I'm right at the corner of the bar (1.4m ahead). Let me do a quick look_around to verify I'm at the correct corner of the bar.

This confirms my position:
- **Ahead**: The bar counter with its rounded corner directly in front of me (1.4m).
- **Right (+90°)**: The pool and lounge chairs — this is the "chairs" area.
- **Behind**: The pool I came past.
- **Left**: Stairs and a passage/exit.

I have walked past the pool (behind me and to the right), walked between the bar (ahead/left) and chairs (right), and I'm now precisely at the corner of the bar counter. This matches the instruction endpoint perfectly: "Stop when you get to the corner of the bar."

I'm within 3 meters of the bar's corner. Let me confirm the STOP.
